name: Playa de Luanco, Mirador de La Formiga y paseo marítimo – ida y vuelta desde Gijón
name_original: Playa de Luanco,  Mirador de La Formiga y paseo marítimo – iday vuelta desde Gijón
region_intro: Road cycling around Gijón/Xixón features a varied terrain, encompassing picturesque coastal paths, rolling inland hills, and river valleys. The region is characterized by its Atlantic coastline with dramatic cliffs and beaches, transitioning to rural areas and forested hills further inland. Cyclists can find well-paved surfaces and routes designed to minimize heavy traffic, providing options for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Gijón/Xixón The most popular road cycling route is Playa España, Tazones y Ría de Villaviciosa desde Gijón, a 42.8 miles (68.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers coastal views and passes by the Villaviciosa estuary. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pico Fario y Alto de Fumarea por Peón – circular desde Gijón, a difficult 43.4 miles (69.9 km) path. This route is known for its significant elevation gain through the hills of Peón. Local road cyclists also love the Playa de Luanco, Mirador de La Formiga y paseo marítimo – ida y vuelta desde Gijón, a 28.1 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Gijón/Xixón is defined by its coastal paths, inland rural areas, and challenging hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult routes with significant climbs. The routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 8,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gijón/Xixón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, from picturesque coastal paths with stunning sea views to challenging inland climbs and serene greenways. You'll find options catering to various fitness levels, often on well-paved surfaces away from heavy traffic.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in the Gijón/Xixón area?
A: Yes, for cyclists seeking demanding rides, Gijón/Xixón provides routes with significant elevation gain. An example is the difficult route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070286892/pico-fario-y-alto-de-fumarea-por-peon-circular-desde-gijon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fario y Alto de Fumarea por Peón – circular desde Gijón&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the hills of Peón.

Q: Can I find family-friendly cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: While many road cycling routes are designed for experienced riders, the region also offers more accessible options. The Vía Verde de La Camocha, an old mining railway, is a family-friendly greenway with tunnels and wooden bridges. You can explore a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070315699/via-verde-de-la-camocha-and-san-andres-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de La Camocha and San Andrés Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; for a moderate experience.

Q: What are some scenic coastal road cycling routes near Gijón/Xixón?
A: The Gijón/Xixón coastline offers beautiful routes with sea views. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077569472/playa-de-luanco-mirador-de-la-formiga-y-paseo-maritimo-ida-y-vuelta-desde-gijon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Luanco, Mirador de La Formiga y paseo marítimo – ida y vuelta desde Gijón&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through coastal areas. Another difficult route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069322554/playa-espana-tazones-y-ria-de-villaviciosa-desde-gijon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa España, Tazones y Ría de Villaviciosa desde Gijón&lt;/a&gt;, offers coastal views and passes by the Villaviciosa estuary.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes will I encounter on road cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Road cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón showcase a variety of natural beauty. You'll experience dramatic cliffs and beaches along the coastline, tranquil river paths like the Piles and Ñora, and rural areas with forests and meadows further inland. Many routes also offer panoramic views from elevated points.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes blend natural beauty with points of interest. You can find stunning views from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1036457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Sol Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5616840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Gijón and the Coast from Pico del Sol&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cider tower and San Lorenzo beach&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Octavian Augustus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: The road cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to challenging hills, and the well-maintained surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Gijón/Xixón area?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes available around Gijón/Xixón on komoot, offering a wide selection for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón benefits from a mild climate, making spring to autumn particularly pleasant for road cycling. During these seasons, you can expect favorable weather conditions to explore the coastal and inland routes.

Q: Are there routes that offer both coastal and inland scenery?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Gijón/Xixón allow you to experience both the Atlantic coastline and the rural, wooded areas further inland. This provides a dynamic cycling experience, transitioning from sea views to green landscapes and traditional villages.

Q: Can I find routes that are circular or loop back to the starting point?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Gijón/Xixón are designed as circular loops. For instance, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070286892/pico-fario-y-alto-de-fumarea-por-peon-circular-desde-gijon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fario y Alto de Fumarea por Peón – circular desde Gijón&lt;/a&gt; is a popular loop that brings you back to Gijón after exploring the hills of Peón.
tips: Always great to drive along here
Splendid views of the Luanco beach, located in the beautiful town of the same name, where you can eat very well, in case you want to make a stop.
Beautiful viewpoint to the beach and port of Candás. An ideal place if we want to take a break while enjoying the views. Access is by paved road.

waypoints.name: View of Candás Beach and Harbor from Mirador de La Formiga
